nodes.label: Approved Shell Command Contract
nodes.rationale: Approved commands run through non-login /bin/sh to preserve ordinary project-script shell semantics without sourcing login profiles.

nodes.label: Minimized Command Environment
nodes.rationale: A small baseline plus explicit opt-in limits accidental propagation of parent-process secrets.

nodes.label: Shell Execution Trust Boundary
nodes.rationale: Service commands intentionally use /bin/sh so trusted project scripts retain their documented shell semantics; this is a trust boundary rather than a sandbox.

nodes.label: Non-Secret Environment Baseline
nodes.rationale: Restricting inherited variables and using fixed system helper paths reduces secret propagation and project-controlled executable substitution.

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Update Verification
nodes.rationale: Missing tooling, provenance, or verification leaves the installed executable unchanged.
